CREATE TABLE test_intervals (id UInt32, timestamp DateTime);
WITH toIntervalMillisecond(1) AS interval_1_second SELECT timestamp + interval_1_second AS result FROM test_intervals;
WITH toIntervalMillisecond(0) AS interval_zero, toDateTime('2024-06-15 12:00:00') AS dt SELECT dt + interval_zero AS result;
WITH toIntervalMillisecond(-1000) AS interval_negative SELECT timestamp + interval_negative AS result FROM test_intervals;
WITH toIntervalMillisecond(1.5) AS interval_float SELECT timestamp + interval_float AS result FROM test_intervals;
WITH toIntervalMillisecond(1000) AS interval_positive SELECT timestamp + interval_positive AS result FROM test_intervals;
WITH (SELECT toIntervalMillisecond(500) AS interval_500) AS subquery SELECT timestamp + interval_500 FROM test_intervals;
CREATE TABLE event_log (event_time DateTime, event_interval IntervalMillisecond);
SELECT event_time + event_interval AS future_time FROM event_log;
WITH (SELECT AVG(event_interval) FROM event_log) AS avg_interval SELECT timestamp + avg_interval AS average_future_time FROM test_intervals;
CREATE TABLE complex_example AS SELECT id, timestamp + toIntervalMillisecond(id * 100) AS adjusted_time FROM test_intervals;
SELECT t.id, t.timestamp, c.adjusted_time FROM test_intervals t JOIN complex_example c ON t.id = c.id;
